attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Place two pairs of keys on the couch.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene contains a dark brown leather couch positioned against a beige wall. On the couch, there is an open, empty cardboard box. The box is light brown and is placed in the center of the couch's seating area.

To the left of the couch, there is a small white side table. On this table, there are two distinct pairs of keys. One pair is silver-colored and has a circular keyring, while the other pair is black with a red keychain attached. Both pairs of keys are resting on the tabletop surface.

Adjacent to the side table is a light teal wooden chair with a spindle back design. The chair is positioned on the beige carpeted floor and is oriented towards the side table and couch.

The floor throughout the scene is covered in a uniform beige carpet.

Relevant to the domain:
- The couch and the cardboard box are not defined as receptacles in the domain file, so they are treated as generic objects.
- The side table is also treated as a generic object.
- The two pairs of keys are objects. One pair has a red keychain, and the other has a circular keyring; these are distinguishable features.
- The robot is not currently at any object’s location.

The goal is to place both pairs of keys on the couch. Currently, both pairs are on the side table. The robot must first navigate to the side table, pick up each pair of keys, and then place them on the couch. The cardboard box on the couch is empty and does not interfere with the task. No receptacles defined in the domain (microwave, fridge, sink) are present or relevant to this task.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_keys_on_couch)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        couch - object
        side_table - object
        cardboard_box - object
        chair - object
        keys_red_keychain - object
        keys_circular_keyring - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot side_table)
        (inReceptacle keys_red_keychain side_table)
        (inReceptacle keys_circular_keyring side_table)
        (inReceptacle cardboard_box couch)
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle keys_red_keychain couch)
            (inReceptacle keys_circular_keyring couch)
        )
    )
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_keys_on_couch)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        couch - object
        side_table - object
        cardboard_box - object
        chair - object
        keys_red_keychain - object
        keys_circular_keyring - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot side_table)
        (inReceptacle keys_red_keychain side_table)
        (inReceptacle keys_circular_keyring side_table)
        (inReceptacle cardboard_box couch)
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle keys_red_keychain couch)
            (inReceptacle keys_circular_keyring couch)
        )
    )
)

To the left of the couch is a small white side table with thin legs. On this table, there is a small black object with red accents (identified as a pair of keys), a small brown circular object (a second pair of keys), and a dark blue rectangular object (possibly a book or case). Next to one remote is a small black object with red accents (a pair of keys), and next to the other remote is a small brown circular object (a second pair of keys). There is also a small green box with a white object inside (possibly a container or case) on the bench.

The instruction is to place two pairs of keys on the couch. Currently, the two pairs of keys are located on the white slatted bench and the side table. The couch is currently empty except for the open cardboard box. The robot must navigate to the locations of the keys, pick them up, and place them on the couch.

Relevant objects for the domain:
- The couch is a receptacle (can hold objects).
- The cardboard box on the couch is a receptacle.
- The side table and slatted bench are not receptacles per the domain definition (they are not listed as microwave, fridge, or sink).
- The keys are objects (not receptacles).
- The remotes and green box are objects.

The robot is not currently at any object’s location and must first navigate to either the slatted bench or the side table to pick up the keys. The keys are not currently on the couch, and the couch is not holding any keys. The robot must perform a sequence of actions to move the keys from their current locations to the couch.

(define (problem place_keys_on_couch)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        couch - receptacle
        cardboard_box - receptacle
        side_table - object
        slatted_bench - object
        keys1 - object
        keys2 - object
        keys3 - object
        keys4 - object
        remote1 - object
        remote2 - object
        green_box - object
        book - object
        chair - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot side_table)
        (inReceptacle keys1 side_table)
        (inReceptacle keys2 side_table)
        (inReceptacle keys3 slatted_bench)
        (inReceptacle keys4 slatted_bench)
        (inReceptacle cardboard_box couch)
        (openable cardboard_box)
        (opened cardboard_box)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle keys1 couch)
            (inReceptacle keys2 couch)
            (inReceptacle keys3 couch)
            (inReceptacle keys4 couch)
        )
    )
)

final.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)

The scene contains a black leather couch positioned against a beige wall, with a small white side table to its left and a light teal wooden chair to its right. On the couch, there is an open, empty cardboard box. The side table holds two pairs of keys (one silver, one black), a small black object resembling a remote or device, and a white rectangular object with a blue screen. The keys are not currently on the couch, which is the target location per the instruction.

The couch is a large, dark-colored piece of furniture with two seat cushions and two backrests, and it is capable of holding objects — thus, it is a receptacle. The side table is also a receptacle, currently holding the keys and other small items. The chair is not a receptacle and cannot hold objects.

The robot is not currently at any object’s location and must first navigate to the side table to pick up the keys. Once the keys are picked up, the robot must move to the couch and place them on it. The box on the couch is open and empty, but it is not relevant to the task unless the instruction were to place the keys inside it — which it is not.

There are no microwaves, fridges, or sinks visible in the scene, so no cleaning, heating, or cooling actions are applicable. No knives are present, so slicing is not relevant. No objects are currently being held by the robot, and none are toggled, cleaned, heated, or cooled.

The goal is to place two pairs of keys on the couch. Currently, both pairs are on the side table. The robot must first go to the side table, pick up one pair of keys, go to the couch, and put them down. Then repeat the process for the second pair. The couch is the target receptacle for this task. The keys are small, distinct objects — one pair is silver-colored, the other black — and they are currently resting on the surface of the side table, not inside any container. The side table is a white, rectangular, flat surface with four thin legs. The couch is large and occupies the central part of the scene, with the box sitting in the middle of its seating area. The teal chair is to the right of the couch and does not interfere with the task. The floor is a uniform beige carpet.
